name: Rowhouse
concept: Rowhouse is the warm-humanist civic system: it feels like the printed annual report of a beloved Philadelphia public library, set in living-room light. The interface treats public records as a shared civic good rather than a sales funnel, so warmth comes from generous reading typography, paper-toned surfaces tinted toward a Philadelphia brick-clay hue, and a candid voice that always shows its work. Credibility without coldness is earned the editorial way: humanist letterforms, ledger-like data alignment, and every number sitting one click from its raw public record.

fonts.justification: Bricolage Grotesque is a contemporary humanist grotesque with subtly irregular, slightly condensed forms that read like crafted civic signage / a museum caption header rather than a SaaS geometric sans, giving warmth and authority without bureaucratic stiffness, and it is explicitly NOT on the reject list (not Inter/DM/Outfit/Jakarta/Space Grotesk/Syne). Source Serif 4 is a warm, highly legible Adobe text serif built for long-form civic and reading material (a well-set public report); it is NOT a banned reflex serif (not Fraunces/Newsreader/Lora/Crimson/Playfair/Cormorant/Garamond/Plex Serif). Asap is a rounded-terminal humanist sans used only for compact UI chrome and table labels where a serif would tire the eye, and is not on the reject list. Martian Mono is a humanist, slightly engineered monospace for parcel IDs, OPA account numbers, and raw-record figures (the candid ledger voice), deliberately not Space Mono or IBM Plex Mono. All four confirmed off every reject list.

brandHue: Hue ~45 (terracotta brick-clay / warm ochre). Justification: Philadelphia is a city of red-brick rowhouses and clay; the brand color is literally the material of the housing stock the tool helps rehab. It is the opposite of generic SaaS blue/indigo and proptech teal, it photographs as warm and human (public-media / library feel), and at high chroma it makes a confident civic accent while at low chroma (~0.006) it tints every neutral toward warm paper. A deep teal-green (hue ~165) serves as the secondary institutional ink for links and the Livability lens, echoing weathered copper and Fairmount Park without veering proptech.
lightTokens: bg oklch(0.975 0.006 75) warm paper; surface oklch(0.992 0.004 80); surface-sunken oklch(0.955 0.007 70); text oklch(0.255 0.012 55) warm near-black ink; muted oklch(0.50 0.014 55); border oklch(0.88 0.008 70); brand oklch(0.585 0.135 45) brick-clay; brand-ink oklch(0.40 0.12 45). Lens colors (categorical, warm-anchored): Price & value oklch(0.55 0.13 250) civic indigo-slate; Development momentum oklch(0.62 0.14 145) growth green; Distress & risk oklch(0.55 0.16 32) warm rust-red (NOT alarm-neon); Livability oklch(0.55 0.10 195) weathered teal.
darkTokens: bg oklch(0.205 0.010 60) warm umber, NOT blue-black; surface oklch(0.245 0.011 58); surface-sunken oklch(0.185 0.009 60); text oklch(0.93 0.008 80) warm paper-white; muted oklch(0.70 0.012 70); border oklch(0.36 0.012 62); brand oklch(0.70 0.115 50) lifted brick so it glows warm on umber; brand-ink oklch(0.80 0.10 52). Lens colors lifted for AA on dark: Price & value oklch(0.70 0.11 250); Development momentum oklch(0.74 0.12 145); Distress & risk oklch(0.70 0.14 34); Livability oklch(0.72 0.085 195). Chroma pulled in near both extremes; neutrals all carry hue 58-80 so dark mode reads as warm lamplit paper, never cold console.
typeScale: Fixed-rem modular scale, ratio 1.25 (major third), few sizes high contrast. --step-data 0.75rem (12px, mono record stamps/labels) — used sparingly; --step-fine 0.8125rem (13px UI labels, Asap); --step-base 1rem (16px Source Serif body, capped 68ch); --step-h3 1.25rem; --step-h2 1.5625rem; --step-h1 1.953rem; --step-display 2.441rem (Bricolage, neighborhood/page titles). Display + h1/h2 in Bricolage Grotesque weight 500-600 with -0.01em tracking; body 400/1.65; labels Asap 500 with +0.02em tracking and sentence case. One mono size for ledger figures.
spacing: 4pt base scale exposed as semantic tokens: --space-1 4px, -2 8px, -3 12px, -4 16px, -6 24px, -8 32px, -12 48px, -16 64px. Layout uses CSS grid/flex with gap (never margin stacks). Deliberately varied rhythm: tight 8px inside ledger rows, generous 48-64px between editorial sections so reading passages breathe like a report. Asymmetric two-column deep-dive (wider record ledger left, narrower context rail right). Content column max 68ch. No blanket card-wrapping; sections separated by hairline rules and whitespace, not boxes.
componentLanguage: No generic rounded-rect-plus-dropshadow card grids. Surfaces are flat paper panels with a single 1px full hairline border (radius 8px) and no shadow; elevation is communicated by tint shift (surface vs surface-sunken), not blur. Lists are LEDGER ROWS: hairline-divided rows, label in Asap on the left, value in Martian Mono right-aligned so figures form a true column you can scan; a lens chip uses a 8px filled dot plus tinted-background pill (full border, text in the lens 900 stop) — NEVER a left side-stripe. Buttons: primary is solid brick brand with paper text; secondary is hairline-bordered transparent with brand-ink text; both 8px radius, hover shifts background tint only, active translateY(1px), no scale bounce. Tables are the hero data object: zebra via faint warm tint on alternate rows, mono numerals, column headers in small-caps Asap, every figure cell carries a hairline-underlined dotted affordance meaning click-to-source. Tabs/lens switcher is an inline segmented control with a moving underline (transform only). Distress score renders as a horizontal stacked decomposition bar (segments are the sub-factors) with an inline legend, not a gauge.
mapTreatment: The map is the hero and the chrome recedes to near nothing. Basemap is a custom desaturated warm-paper cartography: land in bg paper tone, water in a faint weathered-teal wash, streets as thin warm-gray hairlines, almost no labels until you zoom — it reads like a printed civic atlas, not Google/Mapbox default. Choropleth uses ONE active lens at a time so color always means a single thing: each lens has a sequential ramp built from its own hue (e.g. Distress = paper -> warm rust at hue 32, Development = paper -> growth green at 145), low-chroma at the light end so sparse data never screams. Parcel and neighborhood polygons get a 1px brand-tinted stroke on hover and a 2px solid brick selection outline (no glow). A compact lens switcher sits as a floating segmented control bottom-left; a continuous legend with a plain-language caption ("darker = more distress signals, click any band to see what counts") sits bottom-right. Resolution transitions (city->neighborhood->parcel) animate via opacity/scale only, ease-out, reduced-motion shows instant crossfade-free swap.
transparencyEducationExpression: Show-the-work is structural, not decorative. Every figure (assessment, estimate, distress score) is rendered with a dotted-underline affordance and a trailing tiny "source" stamp in Martian Mono linking to the exact public record (OPA, L&I, Records Dept). The rule-based value estimate appears as an expandable plain-English derivation ("3 comps within 0.3mi, median $/sqft x your 1,140 sqft, -4% condition adjustment = $268k") so the math is legible, never a black box. The distress score is a decomposable stacked bar whose segments are the named sub-factors (tax delinquency, open violations, vacancy signal), each segment hoverable to its raw count and record. Teach-in-place uses an inline dotted-underline glossary term that expands a one-sentence definition in a context rail — no modal. A persistent quiet "Where this comes from" line with a data-freshness date sits under each data section, in muted Asap, signaling candor.
communityValueExpression: Neighborhood-impact framing is woven into the data, not bolted on as marketing. Parcel cards and ledgers lead with civic-stakes language: a vacant/distressed parcel surfaces a quiet "Community signal" line ("Vacant 3+ yrs - rehab adds a home back to Fishtown") in warm brand-ink, and the Development momentum lens visualizes net homes-returned and active permits per block so progress is felt at the neighborhood scale. The map's default city view foregrounds neighborhood names as the primary unit (Fishtown, Point Breeze, Kensington) before parcels, reinforcing that the unit of meaning is the community. Aggregate neighborhood stat strips ("142 vacant parcels, 38 in active rehab this year") frame the user as a participant in neighborhood recovery rather than a flipper, and color uses the growth-green lens to make momentum legible and hopeful without celebratory confetti.
